goal: Wave 17.6 — Emergency Card View Restoration + Bug Fixes
summary: Emergency card view restored with redesigned components. Discovered and fixed 3 critical bugs during review cycle: (1) vite.config.js referenced deleted legacy views → Vercel preview failed, (2) EmergencyRedesign.jsx had TDZ ReferenceError from useEffect calling handler before declaration → white screen at runtime, (3) Settings.jsx had unused variable from deleted toggle code. All fixes applied in 4 commits.
lessons_learned: R-010 (hook order) is CRITICAL — not just style, causes runtime TDZ crash
Static analysis cannot detect useEffect→handler TDZ; must test locally
Local quality gates MUST run before push: 18 seconds prevents 30min wasted review
DEVFLOW reviewing without runtime testing missed TDZ bug
new_aps_created: AP-094: Reference handler in useEffect before declaration
AP-095: Skip local quality gates before push
next: Await Gemini Code Assist re-review, then merge PR #452

lessons: Gemini Code Assist naming suggestion was valid and low-risk (9 component exports renamed). Naming consistency (file name = component name) improves codebase readability. check-review workflow successfully processed 1 MEDIUM suggestion end-to-end with inline reply pattern using jq.

findings: S17.6 (commit a66ae22) already deleted RedesignContext.jsx, RedesignContext.js, useRedesign.js. S17.8 scope reduced to JSDoc cleanup only. Removed outdated [data-redesign='true'] scoping reference from Badge component documentation.

findings: Original spec underestimated neon token scope. Audit found: tokens.redesign.css has 0 imports (safe delete); layout.redesign.css and components.redesign.css still imported in index.css (must rename not delete); 250 neon token references in 39 CSS files (deferred to S17.9.5); 3 orphaned CSS files in src/views/ (Emergency.css, Calendar.module.css, LandingPrototype.css). Scope split into S17.9 (file cleanup/rename) and S17.9.5 (neon token migration). Token mapping documented in spec.

goal: CSS Cleanup — File Consolidation & Orphan Deletion (Revised Scope)
aps_triggered: AP-096 (CSS Deletion Without Verifying className Usage)
lessons: CRITICAL: Grep-based orphan verification incomplete. Landing.jsx uses 100+ CSS classes (lp-*) via className attribute, not import statement. Initial deletion caused total layout regression. Fix: Restored file + Landing.jsx import (commit a8667c6). Methodology lesson: Must check BOTH imports AND className usage before deleting CSS. Created AP-096 to prevent recurrence.

Deferred work properly documented: S17.9.5 (neon token migration, 250 refs in 39 files) and S17.9.X/S17.10 (Landing class nomenclature refactor — note: lp-* classes won A/B test, only rename, do not delete).
Gemini Code Assist did not catch the regression initially (visual diff only visible at runtime). Resolved via human visual testing + manual fix + re-push.

wave: Wave H3 — Adapters and Shared Data (Fase 3)
rules_applied: R-091 (consolidate utils, no new api/*.js files)
lessons_learned: DEVFLOW state.json checkpoint protocol is CRITICAL — must update at P0/C0/C2/C5 boundaries, not at session end
Async adapter pattern enables cross-platform storage (web, mobile, server) without implementation variance
Dependency injection prevents platform coupling; all adapters/config are created via factories, zero singletons
next_phase: H3.3 (Schemas & TypedArray) — API schemas for shared data layer. H3.1-H3.2 foundation ready for H3.3.

lessons_learned: createQueryCache factory pattern enables cross-platform reuse without any code change
init() explicit call (not auto-on-import) is the key difference vs legacy queryCache.js
webQueryCache.js is the single init() call site for web — mobile will have its own
removing legacy only after full validation confirms no regressions (R3-006)
cachedServices.js used cachedQuery directly — needed inline alias pattern to bridge old API to new engine

summary: Wave H3 completa — PR #463 aprovado e mergeado. Gemini review: 4 sugestões aplicadas (CACHE_KEYS centralization, generateCacheKey dedup, useDashboardContext hardcoded keys, cachedServices import duplication). All checks passing. Branch deletada. Wave H3 Fase 3 encerrada.

root_cause: react@19.0.0 exact version vs react-test-renderer@^19.2.5 peer requirement
impact: Vercel deployment failed, GitHub Actions blocked until resolved
prevention: R-158 (dependency ranges best practice) + AP-H04 (incident example) added to memory

incident: GitHub Actions npm ci sync failure
root_cause: New workspace added to package.json but package-lock.json not committed after npm install
fix: Commit package-lock.json after local npm install
impact: CI/CD blocked until lock file synced
prevention: R-159 pattern: always commit lock file after monorepo changes

incident: GitHub Actions smoke test fails: React/React-DOM version mismatch
root_cause: Semver ranges ^19.2.0 resolved to react@19.2.5 and react-dom@19.2.3
impact: Smoke test blocked CI until fixed
prevention: R-160: React/React-DOM must be pinned to exact identical versions + AP-H06 incident example

deliverables: Web build continues passing (npm run build)

issues_resolved: AP-H09: registerRootComponent activa expo-router em Expo Go SDK 53
AP-H10: sessão não persiste sem auth-aware navigation
SecureStore chunked storage para tokens >2048 bytes
gsync-native bug: git diff --quiet vs git diff > /dev/null
final_state: App funcional em Expo Go: login, navegação, sessão persistente entre aberturas
key_insight: SecureStore chunked é necessário mas não suficiente para persistência de sessão — Navigation.jsx precisa de getSession() no mount + initialRouteName dinâmico
debugging_methodology: Layer-by-layer isolation (Layer 0=View puro, Layer 1=Navigation, etc) + DevTools log analysis em vez de trial-and-error
